findings.evidence: `lowerFace (C) (v)` at lines 71-73 has no membership hypothesis, while the dimension-drop theorem at lines 170-172 needs `hv : v ∈ C.directions`; `upperFace` at lines 77-80 already requires that hypothesis.
findings.fix: Give `lowerFace` the same present-direction argument as `upperFace` (or separate a raw `eraseDirection` helper from the mathematical face API) and update lower-face exponent/weight wrappers that are meant to be face data to take `hv : v ∈ C.directions`.
findings.issue: `lowerFace` is exposed as a codimension-one face but accepts any `v : V`; if `v ∉ C.directions`, line 73 erases nothing, so the “face” is just `C` and does not drop dimension.

summary: The bundled upper face enforces that the erased direction is present, but the bundled lower face does not. This makes the advertised codimension-one face API semantically too weak at the absent-direction edge case.
